title: Teaching & Open Educational Resources
text: EUR_DATA is built around two modules at University College Dublin that integrate the study of EU politics with hands-on data analytics. The teaching materials below are released as open educational resources under CC-BY-4.0: you are free to reuse and adapt them with attribution.

title: Teaching & Open Educational Resources
text:
Modules

INRL20160 — The Politics of the EU (undergraduate)
An introduction to the institutions, actors, and policy processes of the European Union.



Year
Syllabus




2025-2026

2024-2025
PDF

POL40970 — The Politics of European Governance (postgraduate)
A graduate module on European governance, combining institutional analysis with quantitative methods and project-based work on real EU data.

title: Teaching & Open Educational Resources
section: Working with the data in teaching
text:
Working with the data in teaching
The Commission Formation dataset is designed for classroom use. The worked example notebook in the repository shows how to load and explore the tables: exploration.ipynb.

title: Data & Outputs
text: The EUR_DATA collection provides structured, analysis-ready datasets on European political institutions. Each dataset is released with reproducible pipeline code, a column-level codebook, and a test suite. All data is licensed CC-BY-4.0 (the euandi dataset additionally requires crediting its original authors; see that section).

title: Data & Outputs
text:
Commission Formation (2024-2029)
The formation of the second von der Leyen Commission, from the June 2024 European Parliament elections to the adoption of the 2025 Commission Work Programme. Six interlinked tables, all keyed on a three-letter commissioner_id.








Table
Rows
What it captures




commissioners
27
The College: portfolio, party, gender, and DG assignments


mission_letter_commitments
1057
Policy commitments from 26 presidential mission letters


hearings
26
Confirmation hearing dates, committees, outcomes, and source URLs


investiture_vote
688
MEP-level roll-call votes on the College (27 Nov 2024)


work_programme_items
130
Items from four of the five annexes of the CWP 2025


formation_timeline
13
Key institutional milestones (250-day span)



Notable fields: each commitment carries n_letters_sharing_text and is_boilerplate, flagging text repeated across mission letters (515 of the 1057 rows are boilerplate shared by multiple letters); each hearing row carries a source_url pointing to the EP hearing page.
Downloads: commissioners · commitments · hearings · investiture_vote · work_programme_items · formation_timeline
Every table is also available as XLSX in the same folder.
Documentation: Dataset README · Codebook






Source & attribution. Built from records of the European Parliament Open Data Portal and European Commission documents (mission letters, the Commission Work Programme 2025) via EUR-Lex, © European Union; reuse is permitted under Commission Decision 2011/833/EU.









A citable DOI for this dataset is minted via Zenodo on each release. The DOI will appear here once the first release is published.




Explore the data
The charts below are interactive: hover for exact values.

voteData = FileAttachment("assets/charts/vote_by_group.csv").csv({typed: true})
commitData = FileAttachment("assets/charts/commitments_by_commissioner.csv").csv({typed: true})
annexData = FileAttachment("assets/charts/wp_by_annex.csv").csv({typed: true})
timelineData = FileAttachment("assets/charts/timeline.csv").csv({typed: true})






























Investiture vote by EP political group
The Parliament approved the College by 370 votes to 282, with 36 abstentions. The chart shows how each political group split.

Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 70,
  marginBottom: 40,
  x: {label: "MEPs", grid: true},
  y: {label: null, domain: [...new Set(voteData.map(d => d.ep_party_group))]},
  color: {domain: ["for", "against", "abstain"], range: ["#2c7fb8", "#d73027", "#969696"], legend: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(voteData, {
      y: "ep_party_group",
      x: "count",
      fill: "vote",
      order: ["for", "against", "abstain"],
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})








Mission-letter commitments per commissioner
1057 commitments were extracted from the 26 mission letters: 440 high-confidence (from bullet-point lists) and 617 medium-confidence (from directive sentences and legislative references).

Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 150,
  marginBottom: 40,
  width: 800,
  height: 560,
  x: {label: "Commitments", grid: true},
  y: {label: null, sort: {y: "x", reduce: "sum", reverse: true}},
  color: {domain: ["high", "medium"], range: ["#1a9850", "#a6d96a"], legend: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(commitData, {
      y: "commissioner_name",
      x: "count",
      fill: "confidence",
      order: ["high", "medium"],
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})








Commission Work Programme 2025 by annex
130 items extracted from four of the five annexes of the work programme adopted on 11 February 2025 (COM(2025) 45): 52 new initiatives (Annex I), 37 items from the annual plan on evaluations and fitness checks (Annex II), 37 withdrawals of pending proposals (Annex IV), and 4 envisaged repeals of acts in force (Annex V). The official Annex III (priority pending proposals) is deliberately not extracted here; those procedures are covered by the agenda implementation dataset’s term corpus below.

Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 180,
  marginBottom: 40,
  x: {label: "Items", grid: true},
  y: {label: null, domain: annexData.map(d => d.label)},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(annexData, {y: "label", x: "count", fill: "#542788", tip: true}),
    Plot.text(annexData, {y: "label", x: "count", text: "count", dx: 12, fontWeight: "bold"}),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})








Formation timeline
From the European Parliament elections (6 June 2024) to the adoption of the Commission Work Programme (11 February 2025).

Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 40,
  marginRight: 20,
  marginBottom: 50,
  height: 200,
  x: {label: null, type: "utc", grid: true},
  color: {legend: true, label: "Event type"},
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.dot(timelineData, {
      x: d => new Date(d.date),
      y: 0,
      fill: "event_type",
      r: 6,
      tip: {format: {x: true, y: false}},
      channels: {event: "event_name", institution: "institution"}
    })
  ]
})

title: Data & Outputs
text:
euandi 2024 (European Parliament election)
Voting Advice Application expert coding for the June 2024 European Parliament election: where Irish parties and candidates, and the EU-level party families, placed themselves on 36 policy statements. Two parallel datasets (ie_*, eu_*), final placement only.








Table
Rows
What it captures




ie_parties
19
Irish parties (13) + independent candidates (6)


ie_statements
36
The policy-statement battery (Ireland wording)


ie_positions
684
Each entity’s final position on each statement


ie_salience
57
Each entity’s three most salient statements


eu_parties
10
EU-level political party families


eu_statements
36
The statement battery (EU wording)


eu_positions
360
Each family’s final position on each statement


eu_salience
30
Each family’s three most salient statements



Downloads (Ireland): ie_parties · ie_statements · ie_positions · ie_salience · EU-level: eu_parties · eu_statements · eu_positions · eu_salience
Documentation: Dataset README · Codebook · Summary & figures






Source & attribution. Derived from the euandi 2024 Voting Advice Application coding (euandi 2024 team, European University Institute), published under CC BY 4.0 via EUI Cadmus. Redistributed under CC BY 4.0 with attribution to the original authors. The raw euandi source workbooks are not shipped with this repository; they can be obtained from the EUI Cadmus record above (the dataset README has details).



The position matrix below shows every Irish party and candidate (rows, ordered by similarity) against all 36 statements; the colour is the coded position.



Irish parties: final positions on 36 statements





Irish parties: distribution of final positions

title: Data & Outputs
text:
Commission Agenda Implementation
How far has the Commission delivered the legislative agenda it set itself? Each planned agenda item is linked to its real legislative fate via the EP Open Data API and EUR-Lex, built as a time series (each status row is dated). This release ships Phase 1: the automated Annex IV procedure status and the term corpus; Annex I links and evaluation outcomes are scaffolded for manual curation.








Table
Rows
What it captures




agenda_items
193
The agenda spine: all 130 CWP items + 63 legislative commitments


procedure_references
38
Procedure references parsed from the agenda


procedure_status
38
Dated status snapshot per procedure (the core deliverable)


term_legislative_output
169
Baseline corpus of all 2024-2026 term procedures


evaluations
37
Annex II evaluation tracking (curated)



By source scope, the 193 agenda items split into 52 CWP Annex I new initiatives, 37 Annex II evaluations and fitness checks, 37 Annex IV withdrawals of pending proposals, 4 Annex V envisaged repeals of acts in force, and 63 legislative mission-letter commitments.
Downloads: agenda_items · procedure_references · procedure_status · term_legislative_output · evaluations
Documentation: Dataset README · Codebook · Summary & figures






Source & attribution. Built from records of the European Parliament Open Data Portal and European Commission / EUR-Lex documents, © European Union; reuse is permitted under Commission Decision 2011/833/EU.



The status of the agenda-linked procedures, read against the full procedure ladder. As of the snapshot, all resolved procedures sit at first reading and none have been adopted: the Annex IV items are old proposals pending withdrawal, and a Commission withdrawal is not an EP procedure stage.



Status of agenda-linked procedures



Generated from the dataset pipelines; figures are reproducible via each dataset’s make_summary.py. More datasets will be added as the project develops.

title: About
text:
EUR_DATA (Integrating EU studies and Data Analytics using a Project-based Learning Approach) is an Erasmus+ Jean Monnet Module based at University College Dublin and maintained by the Connected Politics Lab. It is coordinated by James Cross.
The module integrates the study of European Union politics with hands-on data analytics. Students work with real institutional data to build practical quantitative skills, and the structured datasets they help produce are released here as open research resources.
This website hosts three kinds of output:

Data: open, analysis-ready datasets on European political institutions, each with a codebook, reproducible pipeline code, and tests.
Teaching: open educational resources, including module syllabi, slides, and assessment materials.
Code: the full pipelines that build each dataset, on GitHub.

title: About
text:
EUR_DATA (Integrating EU studies and Data Analytics using a Project-based Learning Approach) is an Erasmus+ Jean Monnet Module based at University College Dublin and maintained by the Connected Politics Lab. It is coordinated by James Cross.
The module integrates the study of European Union politics with hands-on data analytics. Students work with real institutional data to build practical quantitative skills, and the structured datasets they help produce are released here as open research resources.
This website hosts three kinds of output:

Data: open, analysis-ready datasets on European political institutions, each with a codebook, reproducible pipeline code, and tests.
Teaching: open educational resources, including module syllabi, slides, and assessment materials.
Code: the full pipelines that build each dataset, on GitHub.

title: About
text:
Licensing and citation
Data and educational resources are released under CC-BY-4.0; code under the Apache License 2.0. Please cite EUR_DATA if you use it; see the CITATION file.

title: About
text:
Contact
For questions, corrections, or feature requests, please open an issue on the GitHub repository.

text:

title: EUR_DATA
text:
EUR_DATA provides structured, analysis-ready datasets, reproducible code, and open educational resources on European political institutions. It is an Erasmus+ Jean Monnet Module based at University College Dublin and maintained by the Connected Politics Lab, integrating the study of EU politics with hands-on data analytics.



Commission Formation (2024-2029): the formation of the second von der Leyen Commission: the College, mission-letter commitments, confirmation hearings, the investiture vote, the 2025 Work Programme, and a formation timeline. Six interlinked tables, 1,941 rows in total.
euandi 2024 (European Parliament election): Voting Advice Application expert coding for the June 2024 EP election, for Irish parties and candidates and for the EU-level party families. Eight tables: 19 Irish entities and 10 EU party families placed on 36 policy statements.
Commission Agenda Implementation: how far the Commission has delivered the legislative agenda it set itself, tracked as a dated time series against the EP Open Data API and EUR-Lex. 193 agenda items, 38 tracked procedures, and a 169-procedure term corpus.

Further datasets will be added as the project develops.




Data: dataset documentation, downloads, and interactive visualisations.
Teaching: open syllabi, slides, and assessment materials.
About: the project, its funding, and how to cite it.




Data and educational resources are released under CC-BY-4.0; code under the Apache License 2.0.

Commission Formation (2024-2029): the formation of the second von der Leyen Commission: the College, mission-letter commitments, confirmation hearings, the investiture vote, the 2025 Work Programme, and a formation timeline. Six interlinked tables, 1,941 rows in total.
euandi 2024 (European Parliament election): Voting Advice Application expert coding for the June 2024 EP election, for Irish parties and candidates and for the EU-level party families. Eight tables: 19 Irish entities and 10 EU party families placed on 36 policy statements.
Commission Agenda Implementation: how far the Commission has delivered the legislative agenda it set itself, tracked as a dated time series against the EP Open Data API and EUR-Lex. 193 agenda items, 38 tracked procedures, and a 169-procedure term corpus.

Further datasets will be added as the project develops.




Data: dataset documentation, downloads, and interactive visualisations.
Teaching: open syllabi, slides, and assessment materials.
About: the project, its funding, and how to cite it.




Data and educational resources are released under CC-BY-4.0; code under the Apache License 2.0.
